title: podman save --compress  does not include tag and name
body:
### Issue Description

`podman save --compress --format docker-dir` does not preserve the image name and tag.

`podman load` produces an image without name and tag.

This works fine however with `oci-archive` or `docker-archive` types.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman pull alpine:latest`
2. `podman save --compress --format docker-dir -o alp alpine:latest`
4. `podman rmi alpine`
5. `podman load -i alp`
6.  `podman images`

Original (pulled):
```
REPOSITORY                   TAG         IMAGE ID     CREATED         SIZE
docker.io/library/alpine     latest      9234e8fb04c4  1 days ago      8.61 MB
```
Restored (loaded):
```
REPOSITORY                   TAG          IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
<none>                       <none>      9234e8fb04c4  1 days ago      8.6 MB
```
[manifest.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21412972/manifest.json)

(also tried with `podman save --compress --format docker-dir -o alp docker.io/library/alpine`)

### Describe the results you received

A restored image without name and tag.

### Describe the results you expected

A restored image with name and tag.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Debian Bookworm 
Kernel 6.12.35+deb13-amd64

### Additional information

 `podman save -o alp.tar alpine:latest`
`podman load -i alp.tar`

This works as expected and the restored image has the correct name and tag.
This only fails with option `--compress --format docker-dir`

comments.body:
man containers-transports says

> An existing local directory path storing the manifest, layer tarballs and signatures as individual files.  This is a non-standardized format, primarily useful for debugging or noninvasive container inspection.

I don't think there is any expectation that the name should be preserved if it is not represented in that format.

The reason it works without compress is because the save/loading preserves the same digest so the storage itself knows it is the same image AFAICT. IF you actually delete the image form your storage and the load the uncompressed version it will also have no name.

So overall I think this is the expected behaviour but I don't know that code part well so I leave to @mtrmac to confirm.

@Luap99 

Hi there, so is it even possible at all to preserve the name and tag with `podman save --compress --format docker-dir`?

Yes, this works as expected. `dir:` is intended to be a ~raw representation of the contents, not a carrier for any extra metadata.

The logical counterpart for that would be to add `podman load --tag-the-only-image=$tag < $input`, letting the consumer of the raw `dir:` input specify a tag. That could potentially be useful for multi-image archive formats like `oci-*` as well (hence the `the-only-image` wording, which would fail on an unexpected multi-image).

Nothing like that exists but it might be interesting to add.

For now, a raw `skopeo copy dir:$dir containers-storage:[$options]$dest:$tag` is an option (but, admittedly, figuring out `$options` and `$dest`, in the non-default cases, is fairly difficult.

> Nothing like that exists but it might be interesting to add.

Yeah I meant if it's currently possible to preserve the tag when saving or assign it during `podman load`, but I'm gathering not right now.

Your solution sounds cool.
I wrote a script that saves the tag and possibly other stuff in `dir/metadata.json` when performing `podman save --compress` and  `podman tag` after restoring with `podman load`. I don't believe that hook scripts are possible (correct me if I'm wrong), so I just run this for saving and loading.

> For now, a raw `skopeo copy dir:$dir containers-storage:[$options]$dest:$tag` is an option (but, admittedly, figuring out `$options` and `$dest`, in the non-default cases, is fairly difficult.

Okay, I'll try this. Thanks for the suggestion.

> skopeo copy dir:$dir containers-storage:[$options]$dest:$tag

I tried this with option `--dest-compress`, but the result was the same.

Can you demonstrate a reproducible example that implements this command to save/compress an alpine image in docker-dir format and restore it with the tag name in tact?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
